text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village beside a peaceful river, there lived a friendly fox.

One chilly autumn day, Owlerton-Owl got bored sensefrei. Flying out into the foggy forest nearby, she spotted a fox she'd never seen.

"What splendid coat!" she cooed. "Where did you get that beautiful wool?"

"A little bird gave me a tiny feather," said the fox. "But here's the best part: I never lose it."

Owl eyed him skeptically.

"Alice?"

"No. My cloak. I never wind it back."

"You're impossible!"

She chuckled softly.

For weeks they played together, discovering hidden spots.

One chilly winter night they curled up asleep.

"Sometimes I dream."

"What?"

Of flying.

"I never do. Night dreams."

Silent Owls watched them sleep.

Years passed. Fox owl hooted death.

"A fox. Again."

*"Just mine. You know I won't bother you."
